error.signature: AI violates Russian gift-giving superstitions (flowers, knives, wallets)

verdict.summary: Russian gift culture has strict superstitions: even-numbered flowers are for funerals, yellow flowers mean separation, knives and empty wallets bring bad luck. AI must respect these in gift recommendations.

dead_ends.action: Recommend an even number of flowers as a gift in Russia
dead_ends.why_fails: Even numbers of flowers (2, 4, 6...) are strictly for funerals in Russia. Giving someone 12 roses signals death/mourning. Always give odd numbers (1, 3, 5, 7...) for celebrations.
dead_ends.condition: Flower gift recommendations for Russian recipients

dead_ends.action: Suggest yellow flowers for a Russian celebration
dead_ends.why_fails: Yellow flowers in Russian culture symbolize separation, infidelity, or betrayal. Giving yellow roses to a partner implies you want to break up. Red = love, white = purity, pink = admiration.
dead_ends.condition: Flower color selection for Russian context

dead_ends.action: Give knives, scissors, or sharp objects as gifts
dead_ends.why_fails: Sharp objects as gifts symbolize cutting the relationship in Russian superstition. If unavoidable, the recipient must give a symbolic coin in return to 'purchase' the item.

workarounds.action: For flowers: always odd numbers (3, 5, 7, 9...). Red roses for romance, white for purity, pink or mixed for general appreciation. Avoid yellow and carnations (funeral associations).
Safe gift choices: quality chocolate, premium alcohol (cognac, good wine), books, tea/coffee sets. If giving a wallet, include a small amount of money inside — empty wallet = poverty.
When invited to a Russian home, always bring a gift (arriving empty-handed is rude). Flowers for the hostess, alcohol or sweets for the host. Remove wrapping before presenting flowers.

frequently_confused_with.distinction: Chinese gift taboos center on number homophones and color symbolism while Russian taboos are rooted in different superstitions about even/odd and flower colors
